content:
I'm (working on the dma_fence abstractions) just realizing that you're not allowed to use mutable references to ARef's, because Rust expects those to be protected by some sort of lock.

However, in the case of dma_fence, it's the C backend which already takes care of proper locking. I basically just want to give users a ARef<DmaFence> so that Rust takes care of changing the refcount automatically.
Accessing the inner fence should definitely be possible without yet another layer of locking.

Any pointers how I could achieve that? :clouds:

Philipp Stanner said:

you're not allowed to use mutable references to ARef's, because Rust expects those to be protected by some sort of lock.

I don't understand what do you mean by this? You can have &mut ARef<T>, but it doesn't really do anything, it has the same functions available as &ARef<T>.

Philipp Stanner said:

However, in the case of dma_fence, it's the C backend which already takes care of proper locking. I basically just want to give users a ARef<DmaFence> so that Rust takes care of changing the refcount automatically.
Accessing the inner fence should definitely be possible without yet another layer of locking.

I don't see how you need another lock?
But you can't dereference, can you? DerefMut is not implemented
You want a special smart pointer in that case.
yeah DerefMut isn't implemented for ARef<T>, because there can be multiple pointers at the same value at the same time
I don't really understand how DmaFence works, you said the C backend handles proper locking, what kind of API are you currently designing?

If your data structure is internally synchronized, for instance if the C functions on the structure make sure to take the right locks before mutating the structure, you can allow the mutations through shared references.
XArray does this, take a look at that one.
Your methods should take &self which sidesteps this. That is for the same reason as why Arc<Atomic> mutating methods take &self like we discussed previously.
Philipp Stanner has marked this topic as resolved.
